Name: Adrienne Kowalski
Education: B.S. Statistics, University of Michigan, 2014. M.S. Data Science, Carnegie Mellon University, 2016. Graduated with distinction; thesis focused on Bayesian inference methods for sparse high-dimensional datasets.
Work Experience: Senior Data Analyst, Meridian Health Systems (2019 - present): leads a team of four analysts producing weekly operational dashboards and quarterly outcome reports for executive leadership. Previously Data Analyst II, Vantage Analytics Group (2016 - 2019): built ETL pipelines in Python and SQL serving three Fortune 500 client accounts.
Notes: Strong communication skills noted by panel. Expressed interest in mentoring junior staff. Salary expectation slightly above posted band; may be negotiable.

Name: Marcus Delgado
Work Experience: Data Analyst, Pinnacle Retail Solutions (2013 - 2021): primary analyst for inventory forecasting models used across 140 store locations. Reduced overstock costs by an estimated 18% over three years through improved demand-signal modeling. Freelance consultant (2021 - present): serves for small-to-mid-size e-commerce clients with custom reporting and A/B test analysis.

Name: Priya Nambiar
Education: B.Tech. Computer Science, IIT Bombay, 2013. M.S. Applied Mathematics, Georgia Tech, 2015. Ph.D. (ABD) Computational Statistics, Georgia Tech. Withdrew to pursue industry role in 2017 after completing all coursework and qualifying exams.
Notes: Referred by current employee Tomas Ferreira. Panel flagged potential culture-fit concern around remote-work flexibility expectations. Recommend follow-up conversation before advancing.

Name: Jordan Whitfield
Work Experience: Business Intelligence Developer, Cascadia Energy (2015 - 2022): designed and maintained a suite of 60+ Tableau dashboards consumed by 200+ internal users; managed migration from on-premise data warehouse to Snowflake. Lead Analyst, Novex Financial (2022 - present): owns end-to-end reporting cycle for regulatory filings, coordinating across compliance, engineering, and finance teams.
Notes: Self-taught; no formal degree. Provided an impressive portfolio of published Tableau public workbooks and a GitHub repository with dbt models. Panel unanimously agreed credentials are more than sufficient given demonstrated output.
